title: Turning science into music to find common ground and inspire￼
summary: If climate change was a song, what would it sound like? Alaska composer Michael Bucy has an answer to that question. Warming oceans, melting ice and disrupted jet streams came to life in his original composition performed in Juneau earlier this winter. Complex changes in the Bering Sea inspired the musical representation, which Bucy set to music with the help of University of Alaska Fairbanks scientist Vladimir Alexeev. title: Corn and soybean production up in 2021, USDA Reports: Corn and soybean stocks up from year earlier, winter Wheat Seedings up for 2022
summary: Increased acreage and higher yields for corn and soybeans led to record high soybean production and near-record high corn production, according to the 2021 Crop Production Annual Summary released today by the U.S. Department of Agriculture’s National Agricultural Statistics Service (NASS).

title: Voices of Alaska: Davyd Betchkal
summary: Davyd Betchkal is a soundscape specialist with the National Park Service in Alaska. The post Voices of Alaska: Davyd Betchkal appeared first on Alaska Magazine .
